cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Sally is a girl and she has 3 brothers. So, her brothers are three in number. Now, each of these brothers has 2 sisters. Hmm, the question is asking how many sisters Sally has. Wait, so if each brother has 2 sisters, does that mean there are two girls in the family besides Sally? Or is there something I'm missing here? Let me think. Sally has sisters, so the sisters would be the other female siblings. If each brother has 2 sisters, that would include Sally herself and her sisters. So if each brother has 2 sisters, then Sally plus one more sister. That would mean Sally has one sister. Because Sally is one of the sisters each brother has.
